root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Developers Guide
root.summary: Introductory material and navigation index for the Cúram 8.1.2 Provider Management Developers Guide. It states the guide is for developers customizing CPM, distinguishes customization from configuration, and points to main extension areas: strategy-pattern service interfaces with Guice replacement modules, event hooks, workflow process definitions, products and rule sets, financial payment types, service deliveries, and compliancy/miscellaneous entities. The guide also includes Appendix A and Appendix B with XML schema definitions for import fragments (UseReference, RelatedConcept, ExternalTerm). The first pages are notice/legal content, including usage permissions, warranty disclaimer, export-law note, privacy policy, and trademarks. This node is useful as a table-of-contents-style hub for locating CPM customization APIs and related documentation.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory notice stating that users should read the “Notices” section on page 65 before using the information or the supported product. Includes Merative copyright information (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Introductory “Edition” and Table of Contents pages for the Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 provider management documentation. It states the purpose, audience, and scope of Provider Management customization versus configuration. The main content begins with customization via strategy patterns, events, workflows, products, rule sets, and financials. Tables list key customizable interfaces for provider, placement/contract, training, and service invoice implementations, including default behaviors and extension points. Useful as a navigation hub for CPM customization topics.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-24
children.summary: This section lists CPM provider-management customization points on pages 15–24. It covers replaceable interfaces for payment/rate logic and roster processing (DeterminePaymentAmount, ApplicableRateListener/Processor, RateDetermination, roster submission due dates, roster matching, voucher validation, attendance payment determination, PRLI unit allocation). It then enumerates many business events for custom processing before/after actions across Providers, Provider Approval Checks, Enquiries, Licenses, Home Studies, Compartments, Places, Requests, Member Certifications, Deductions, Provider Offerings, Provider Groups, Service Offerings/Groups, and Service Authorizations.

children.title: Edition — pages 25-34
children.summary: This section catalogs Provider Management customization-point events across pages 25–34. It lists before/after event classes and descriptions for Service Authorization, Service Authorization Line Item, Placement, Reservation, Contract Version, Flat Rate Contract, Utilization Contract, Service Invoice Line Item, payment/reassessment, correction, and Attendance/Provider Roster Line Item interfaces. Useful for finding specific event hooks, related interface names, and the action that raises each event (create, modify, approve, terminate, retrieve, etc.).

children.title: Edition — pages 35-44
children.summary: Covers CPM customization points for attendance, financial, referral, service delivery, and workflow processes. Lists many provider management event hooks (rosters, PRLI correction, payment frequency, service offering attendance, financial summaries, over/under payments, payment processing, nominee reassignment, rate validation, referral notifications, service delivery submission/evaluation) with before/after triggers and implementation classes. Also documents workflow definitions and source XML locations for External Enquiry, Home Study Approval, New Invoice Created, Service Invoice Exception Processing, and starts Service Invoice Line Item Approval, including default manual-activity behavior and key raised events.

children.title: Edition — pages 45-54
children.summary: This section documents several Provider Management workflows and their defaults: invoice line item approval/correction, supervisor request decision, external user notification, roster exception processing, roster line item approval/correction, and new client added to roster. It lists enactment conditions, calling methods, XML source locations, manual activity allocation strategies, and resulting events. It then introduces CPM products and rule sets, explaining the four provider products, related DMX files, notification exclusion behavior, and how to customize it by extending NotificationExcludedProviderCaseTypesImpl and binding it in a module.

children.title: Edition — pages 55-64
children.summary: Covers CPM/Provider Management configuration and extensions. Includes sample allocation strategy code for excluding cases from notification by case type or product delivery type. Describes CPM rule sets and financials for provider invoice, placement, flat-rate contract, and attendance payments. Explains service deliveries using product delivery processing, creation of product delivery cases, and display of product delivery info. Notes unsupported CPM sample packages and miscellaneous entities. Ends with Appendix XML mapping/schema definitions for taxonomy import fragments.

Introductory chapter for Cúram CPM/Provider Management customization. Defines scope and audience, then covers strategy-pattern extension points with replaceable interfaces for provider, placement/contract, training, service invoice, rates, and roster logic. It also catalogs event hooks across providers, service authorizations, placements, contracts, attendance, financials, referrals, and service delivery, plus workflow definitions, products/rule sets, financial payment types, service deliveries, compliance notes, and XML schema appendices. Good navigation hub for locating CPM customization and extension APIs.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This section is a legal notice covering sample data disclaimers, copyright/license terms for sample programs, a link to Merative’s privacy policy, and trademark attributions. It states that example names are fictitious, sample code is provided “AS IS” without warranty or liability, and lists trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: This section contains legal notices: sample data are fictitious, sample programs may be copied/modified/distributed under the Merative Client Agreement, and the examples are provided “AS IS” without warranty. It also links to Merative’s privacy policy and lists trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft/Windows, and UNIX, plus a copyright notice for Merative US L.P. (2012, 2024).

Legal notice and usage terms for the publication: personal noncommercial use and internal enterprise use are allowed only if proprietary notices are kept; redistribution, derivative works, and external use need Merative consent. It disclaims all warranties (“AS-IS”), limits liability, notes possible patents, export-law compliance, and that non-Merative product info and external websites are not endorsed or guaranteed. Copyright: Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for a Cúram Provider Management guide. Lists major sections on developing with Provider Management, including strategy patterns, event-based customization points, workflow process definitions, products and rule sets, financials, service deliveries, and compliancy. Also includes appendices for schema definitions of XML fragments used by the import process, plus notices, privacy policy, and trademarks. Useful as a navigation index rather than substantive content.

children.title: 1 Developing with Provider Management
1.1 Using Strategy Patterns to Customize CPM
Provider Implementations
children.summary: This section lists CPM provider implementation extension points that developers can customize via service layer interfaces and replacement Guice modules. It distinguishes customization from configuration and notes that default implementations exist. The table covers provider enrollment date handling, provider/group/enquiry/license reference number generation, home study recommendation approval security, and provider offering approval criteria. It is useful if you need the exact interfaces and customization targets for provider-related behavior.

children.title: Placement and Contract Implementations
children.summary: This section lists customizable provider-management interfaces and what they control. It covers ServiceOfferingValidation for service validation and backdating service-offering start dates, ExternalUserPasswordStrategy for initial and reset password generation for external users, and ProviderMemberOfferingTrainingCriteria for changing approval behavior when training requirements are incomplete or waived. It is a table of extension points, useful for seeing which implementations agencies can replace or customize.

children.title: Training Implementations
children.summary: This section lists customizable implementation strategies in Cúram 8.1.2, including PlacementPaymentStrategy for deciding whether placements are paid by invoice or placement, FlatRateContractCoverPatternStrategy for contract payment cover-period patterns, and ReferenceNumberGenerator for generating contract reference numbers. It notes the default implementation classes and explains that new implementations can change these mechanisms for specific business rules.

children.title: Service Invoice Implementations
children.summary: This section lists customizable Service Invoice implementations in Cúram. It covers three extension points: ServiceInvoiceLineItemValidationStrategy for validating invoice line-item units, ServiceInvoicePaymentStrategy for managing and potentially redirecting service invoice payments, and ServiceInvoiceLineItemValidator for validating invoice line items themselves. Each has a default implementation and can be replaced to change invoice validation or payment behavior, such as blocking line items when an invoice is In Progress.

children.title: Custom Rates and Reassessment
children.summary: Describes custom rate reassessment support in provider management. The main interface, curam.financial.impl.ApplicableRateListener, handles re-assessment of payments for service authorization line items, placements, service invoice line items, and provider roster line items. It has two `reAssess` APIs: one taking a Service Authorization Line Item plus date range, used when only the authorization is known; another taking Delivery Evidence Information plus date range, used when product-level details are available. Both route to ApplicableRateProcessor to apply rate changes and recalculate payments.

children.title: Roster Implementations
children.summary: This section lists customizable Roster implementations in Cúram Provider Management. It covers interfaces for generating rosters and validating roster items: DetermineRosterSubmissionDueDate for calculating roster submission due dates (default impl provided, can change grace-period logic), MatchProviderRosterLineItem for validating/matching roster line item details, and VoucherValidator for matching/validating voucher details. It explains that agencies can replace defaults to apply custom, program-specific validation or business-day-based due date logic.

This section explains how CPM can be customized via service-layer strategy interfaces, default implementations, and replacement Guice modules. It distinguishes customization from configuration and lists key extension points for provider setup, service/placement/contract behavior, training approvals, service invoices, rate reassessment, and roster handling. Examples include reference number generation, approval/security criteria, payment strategy, invoice validation, rate recalculation, and roster due-date/matching logic. Useful if you need the specific interfaces and replacement points for changing CPM business behavior.

children.title: 1.2 Using Events to Add Custom Processing to CPM
Provider Customization Points
(deprecated) Provider Events
children.summary: Deprecated Provider Events for CPM custom processing. This section points to events on the `curam.provider.impl.Provider` interface and explains that business events can be used at extension points to add logic before, after, or both around actions. It appears under provider customization points and is mainly an index/reference entry to provider event details rather than a full event list.

children.title: Provider Enquiry Events
children.summary: Lists Provider Enquiry events in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderEnquiry`, for use before/after key enquiry actions. The table covers `ProviderEnquiryCloseEvents` (when a provider enquiry is closed), `ProviderEnquiryTransferEnquiryToProviderEvents` (when a provider is enrolled from an enquiry), and `ProviderEnquirySetProviderEnquiryDetailsEvents` (when an enquiry is created). Useful if you need event hooks tied to provider enquiry lifecycle methods.

children.title: License Events
children.summary: Lists license-related event hooks in curam.provider.impl.License, with before-and-after events for suspend, reject, and approve actions. The page is part of a broader event reference that also introduces home study, compartment, and place events, but this section specifically covers License Event Details and the methods they correspond to: License.suspend(), License.reject(), and License.approve().

children.title: Home Study Events
children.summary: Lists Home Study events in the curam.homestudy.impl.HomeStudy interface. It documents three before-and-after event classes: HomeStudyApproveEvents for approving a provider home study recommendation, HomeStudySubmitEvents for submitting a home study, and HomeStudyRejectEvents for rejecting a home study recommendation. Useful if you need event hooks or lifecycle points for home study processing.

children.title: Compartment Events
children.summary: This section lists Compartment events in the Cúram 8.1.2 provider/place event reference. It identifies the event class `CompartmentCloseEvents`, which is raised before and after a compartment is closed via `curam.place.impl.Compartment.close()`. It appears within a broader table of event details and follows adjacent sections for License and Home Study events.

children.title: Place Events
children.summary: This section lists event classes for provider, license, home study, compartment, and place-related operations in Cúram 8.1.2. It names event interfaces and when they fire, including before/after update, suspend, reject, approve, submit, and close actions. The Place Events subsection indicates events located in the `curam.place.impl.Place` interface, but the excerpt ends before its specific event table.

children.title: Request Events
children.summary: Lists Request events in curam.externaluseraccess.impl.Request. Table 14 defines before/after events for request lifecycle actions on externally created requests: RequestAcceptEvents for accept(), RequestSubmitEvents for submit(), and RequestRejectEvents for reject(). This section sits among neighboring event tables for Place and Member Certification events and is useful for locating request-related event hooks in the provider management API.

children.title: Member Certification Events
children.summary: Lists member certification event hooks in `curam.provider.impl.MemberCertification`. It includes events raised before and after a certification is modified, and when the derived status of a provider member certification is retrieved. This section is a small event-reference table within the broader provider management documentation.

children.title: Provider Deduction Events
children.summary: Lists provider-management event classes and the interfaces/methods that raise them. This page covers Place events, Request events, and Member Certification events, each describing before/after event hooks for actions like close, mark out of use/in use, occupied, get location, accept/submit/reject requests, and modify/retrieve certification status. It then introduces “Provider Deduction Events” as being located in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderDeduction`, but the actual deduction event details are not shown on this page.

children.title: Provider Offering Events
children.summary: Defines provider offering event hooks in Cúram 8.1.2. Covers `ProviderOffering` interface events raised before/after approval, denial, approval criteria checks, and contract retrieval, plus `ProviderOfferingUtil` event for retrieving a provider offering by service offering and provider. Also notes adjacent provider deduction event details above and the start of provider offering rate events below. Useful for developers seeking event class names, trigger points, and associated implementation interfaces.

children.title: Provider Offering Rate Events
children.summary: This section lists provider offering rate events in Cúram 8.1.2, part of the event tables for provider-related APIs. It identifies where events are defined and when they fire, including provider offering approval/denial, approval-criteria checks, contract retrieval, and retrieval by service offering and provider. It also shows that the next event group is in `curam.providerservice.impl.ProviderOfferingRate`. Useful if you need event names, descriptions, or API locations for provider offering/rate integration.

This section introduces CPM provider customization points and explains that business events can be used before, after, or both around extension-point actions. It then catalogs event hooks for provider-related interfaces: deprecated Provider events, ProviderApprovalCheck, ProviderEnquiry, License, HomeStudy, Compartment, Place, Request, MemberCertification, ProviderDeduction, ProviderOffering, and ProviderOfferingUtil. Each table names the event classes and the interface methods that trigger them, making this a reference node for locating lifecycle/event hooks across provider management APIs.

children.title: Provider Group Customization Points
Provider Group Events
children.summary: Lists provider group customization points and the Provider Group event hooks in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroup`. Covers events raised before and after closing, enrolling, reopening, and retrieving common approved provider service offerings for a group. Also begins the Provider Group Associate events in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroupAssociate`, including removal of a provider from a provider group. Useful if you need extension points tied to provider group lifecycle and retrieval operations.

children.title: Provider Group Associate Events
children.summary: Lists provider-management customization events for Provider Groups and Provider Group Associates. Specifically covers provider group lifecycle events (close, enroll, reopen, get common approved service offerings) and the associate event for removing a provider from a provider group. Notes these events are defined in the curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroup and curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroupAssociate interfaces and are raised before and after the related operations.

Lists provider group customization points in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroup` and `curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroupAssociate`. Covers before/after events for provider group close, enroll, reopen, and retrieval of common approved service offerings, plus the associate event for removing a provider from a group. Useful for finding extension hooks around provider group lifecycle and related retrieval/removal operations.

children.title: Service Offering Customization Points
Service Offering Events
children.summary: Lists provider-management event customization points. Covers provider offering rate modification events, provider group events (close, enroll, reopen, and retrieval of common approved service offerings), and provider group associate events for removing a provider from a group. Also introduces service offering customization points and notes that service offering events are defined in the `curam.serviceoffering.impl.ServiceOffering` interface.

children.title: Service Group Events
children.summary: Lists Service Group event hooks in `curam.serviceoffering.impl.ServiceGroup`. The section covers four before/after events: adding a Service Offering to a Service Group, removing a Service Offering, retrieving Service Offerings from a Service Group, and retrieving a Service Group by reference. Useful if you need event names, trigger points, or related Service Group customization/API details.

Introduces service offering customization points. It lists provider-management event hooks such as provider offering rate modification, provider group events for close/enroll/reopen and retrieval of common approved service offerings, plus provider group associate removal events. The section also notes that service offering events are defined in the `curam.serviceoffering.impl.ServiceOffering` interface and that related provider group events are defined in `curam.provider.impl.ProviderGroup` and `ProviderGroupAssociate`.

children.title: Service Authorization Customization Points
Service Authorization Events
children.summary: Introductory section for Service Authorization customization points. It states that the following events are located in the `curam.serviceauthorization.impl.ServiceAuthorization` interface and introduces Table 24: Service Authorization Event Details. The excerpt itself does not list the individual Service Authorization events yet, but appears immediately after Service Offering and Service Group event tables. Open this node if you need the available Service Authorization event hooks and related extension points.

children.title: Service Authorization Line Item Events
children.summary: Lists Service Authorization Line Item event interfaces in `curam.serviceauthorization.impl.ServiceAuthorizationLineItem`. Covers before/after events for closing, inserting, modifying, canceling, and retrieving derived status of a Service Authorization Line Item. The page also shows nearby Service Authorization event classes, but this section specifically focuses on line item events and their triggers.

Lists Service Authorization customization points in `curam.serviceauthorization.impl`. It introduces Table 24 with events for finding line items by service provision details, adding line items, inserting service authorizations, adding/deleting vouchers, retrieving derived status, and generating SALIs by frequency/anchor date. It then presents Table 25 for `ServiceAuthorizationLineItem` events: close, insert, modify, cancel, and derived status retrieval, all raised before and after. Open this node for the available Service Authorization event hooks and extension points.

children.title: Placement Customization Points
Placement Events
children.summary: Placement Events for `curam.place.impl.Placement` are listed here. The section defines event classes raised before and after key placement actions, including `PlacementTransferClientEvents` for transferring a client to a new place via `Placement.transferClient()`, and `PlacementTransferClientToReservationEvents` for transferring a client and creating a reservation for the new place. Useful if you need customization hooks around client transfer and reservation creation in Placement.

children.title: Reservation Events
children.summary: Lists Reservation event classes in curam.reservation.impl.Reservation and when they fire: expire, createReservation, confirmPlacement, updateReservation, cancelOverlap of active reservations, and getPlaceAvailableInDateRange. Each event is raised before and after the corresponding reservation action. This section is a reference table of reservation-related integration events and is useful if you need event hooks for reservation lifecycle handling.

This section introduces Placement customization points and lists event hooks in `curam.place.impl.Placement`. It documents two before/after event classes: `PlacementTransferClientEvents` for `Placement.transferClient()` when transferring a client to a new place, and `PlacementTransferClientToReservationEvents` for transferring a client while creating a reservation for the new place. Useful if you need integration or customization around placement transfers and related reservation creation.

children.title: Contract Customization Points
Contract Version Events
children.summary: Lists contract-related event hooks in Cúram 8.1.2. Covers `curam.contracts.impl.ContractVersion` events for printing, previewing, and validating contracted provider offering rates and place limits; `ContractVersionProviderOffering` events for copying non-contracted rates to contracts, creating default rates, checking duplicate provider offerings on live contracts, and creating contracted rates; and `FlatRateContract` events for activation and re-editing of flat rate contracts. Useful for finding exact event class names and associated contract lifecycle actions.

children.title: Flat Rate Contract Events
children.summary: Defines flat rate contract lifecycle events in `curam.contracts.impl.FlatRateContract`. The table lists events raised before and after activation and re-editing of a flat rate contract, including `FlatRateContractActivateEvents` during `activate()` and `FlatRateContractReEditEvents` during `reEdit()`. This section is useful if you need event hook points for flat rate contract activation or editing.

children.title: Utilization Contract Events
children.summary: Lists Utilization Contract event hooks in `curam.contracts.impl.UtilizationContract`, with before/after events for delete, generate, activate, terminate, renew, re-edit, clone, clone for renewal, and amend operations. Each event is tied to the corresponding contract method. The section also transitions into Service Invoice customization points and introduces Service Invoice events in `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoice`.

Lists contract customization event hooks in Cúram 8.1.2. Covers `curam.contracts.impl.ContractVersion` events for printing, previewing, and validating contracted provider offering rates and place limits; `ContractVersionProviderOffering` events for copying non-contracted rates, creating default/contracted rates, and checking duplicates on live contracts; and `FlatRateContract` events for activation and re-editing of flat rate contracts. Useful for finding exact event class names and lifecycle method trigger points.

children.title: Service Invoice Customization Points
Service Invoice Events
children.summary: Introduces Service Invoice events in the `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoice` interface, part of the Service Invoice customization points. This section is a table of event details showing which event classes are raised before and after service invoice operations, helping developers hook custom behavior into invoice processing.

children.title: Service Invoice Line Item Events
children.summary: Lists service invoice line item event classes in Cúram 8.1.2 and the interfaces they belong to. Covers approve/deny/submit, matching events for case, payee, provider, client, service authorization retrieval/validation, payment generation and amount determination, reassessment, flat-rate contract matching, amount paid retrieval, and payment option changes. Also includes helper and transaction-helper events for matching and creating cancellation/denial/invoiced transactions. Useful as a reference index of event hooks and related implementation methods.

children.title: Service Invoice Line Item Correction Events
children.summary: Lists Service Invoice Line Item Correction events in `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemCorrection`: before/after approve, deny, submit, and validate line item against authorization. Briefly transitions into Attendance customization points, introducing Provider Roster Line Item events such as modify, daily attendance modification, approve, and add client. Useful as an index entry for event hooks related to service invoice corrections and attendance roster processing.

Lists Service Invoice customization points for Cúram 8.1.2. Covers event hook tables for `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoice`, `ServiceInvoiceLineItem`, `DeterminePaymentAmount`, `PaymentOptionProcessor`, `ServiceInvoiceLineItemHelper`, and `ServiceInvoiceLineItemTransactionHelper`. Includes events for adding/bulk approving invoice line items, retrieving invoice status, line item approve/deny/submit, matching case/payee/provider/client, service authorization validation/retrieval, payment generation and amount determination, reassessment, payment option changes, and creation of cancellation/denial/invoiced transactions. Useful as an index of customization event classes and related methods.

children.title: Attendance Customization Points
Provider Roster Line Item Events
children.summary: Lists Attendance customization events for `curam.attendance.impl.ProviderRosterLineItem` (and related processing). Covers roster line item lifecycle hooks: modify on SALI modification, modify, daily attendance updates, approve/submit/deny, submit from roster, submit-and-approve correction, client accommodation, expected unit calculation and updates, configuration retrieval, exception task viewing, correction indicator/header/pay-based-on-attendance/absence/daily attendance/original details access. Also notes the next interface, `curam.attendance.impl.AttendanceInformationProcessing`, starts a separate table.

children.title: Provider Roster Line Item Correction (PRLI Correction) Events
children.summary: Lists provider roster line item-related event hooks in the attendance module. It covers events for retrieving roster line items for cases and clients, matching clients/cases/vouchers, and creating denial, cancellation, and provider roster line item transactions. It also introduces the Provider Roster Line Item Correction (PRLI Correction) events in the curam.attendance.impl.PRLICorrection interface, but the excerpt shows only the section/table heading, not the individual event names.

children.title: Roster Events
children.summary: Lists Roster Events in curam.attendance.impl.RosterProcessing, with before/after event hooks for generating a blank roster manually, retrieving applicable roster range, and creating rosters for Service Authorization Line Items, including overlapping-date cases. Also shows related nearby event tables for Provider Roster Line Item Correction and Attendance Payment Frequency, indicating this page is part of a broader attendance event reference.

children.title: Attendance Payment Frequency Events
children.summary: Defines Attendance Payment Frequency events in `curam.attendance.impl.AttendancePaymentFrequency`. The only listed event class is `AttendancePaymentFrequencyGetDerivedStatusEvents`, raised before and after the status of an attendance payment configuration entry is retrieved. This section sits between roster events and service offering attendance configuration events and is useful for finding event hooks related to attendance payment configuration status lookup.

children.title: Service Offering Attendance Configuration Events
children.summary: Lists event hooks for attendance-related configuration and processing in Cúram. This section covers Provider Roster Line Item Correction events (approve, deny, submit), RosterProcessing events (manual roster generation, applicable roster range retrieval, overlapping-date roster creation, roster creation), and AttendancePaymentFrequency status retrieval. It then introduces Service Offering Attendance Configuration events in the curam.attendance.impl.SOAttendanceConfiguration interface, indicating this page is mainly an event reference for attendance configuration workflows.

children.title: Service Offering Attendance Payment Events
children.summary: Defines the Service Offering Attendance Payment event customization point. It lists the event SOAttendancePaymentGetDerivedStatusEvents in curam.attendance.impl.SOAttendancePayment, raised before and after a Service Offering Attendance Payment status is retrieved. The section also transitions into Financial Customization Points and introduces financial event interfaces, but the main focus here is the attendance payment event detail table.

Attendance Customization Points catalogs attendance-related event hooks. It covers ProviderRosterLineItem lifecycle and retrieval events: modify, daily attendance, approve/submit/deny, correction submission, client accommodation, expected unit calculation/update, configuration lookup, exception viewing, and getter events for correction indicator, case header, pay-based-on-attendance, absence, daily attendance, and original details. It also includes AttendanceInformationProcessing retrieval events for roster line items by case/client, ProviderRosterLineItemHelper matching events, ProviderRosterLineItemTransactionHelper transaction creation events, PRLICorrection approve/deny/submit events, RosterProcessing events, and AttendancePaymentFrequency status retrieval.

children.title: Financial Customization Points
Financial Events
children.summary: This section lists Financial customization events in `curam.financial.impl`, covering multiple APIs and interfaces. It includes events for retrieving service delivery summary information, listing reassessment results, generating over/under payments for provider roster and service invoice line items, payment processing steps, determining payee details, and creating case nominees for provider cases. The content is a reference table of event class names and what triggers them, useful for integration or event-hook customization.

Lists Financial customization points in `curam.financial.impl`. Covers event hooks on `FinancialAPI`, `GenerateOverUnderPayment`, `PaymentProcessing`, and `ProcessCaseNominee`. Events are raised before/after retrieving service delivery summary information, listing reassessment results, generating over/under payments for Provider Roster and Service Invoice line items, processing payments, determining payee details, and creating case nominees for provider cases. Useful as a reference table of event class names and trigger points for integration or customization.

children.title: Referral Customization Points
Referral Events
children.summary: This section lists referral-related event customization points in the `curam.referral.impl.Referral` interface. It appears to be part of the Provider Management / Financial Event Details documentation and focuses on events raised before and after referral processing actions. Useful for developers looking for referral event hooks or extension points.

This section introduces referral customization points in the `curam.referral.impl.Referral` interface. It identifies referral events as extension hooks raised before and after referral processing actions. The excerpt is brief and mainly signals where developers can find referral event customization, likely within Provider Management documentation. Useful if you need referral-specific event hooks, interception points, or extension behavior.

children.title: Service Delivery Customization Points
Service Delivery Events
children.summary: Lists Service Delivery customization events in Cúram 8.1.2. It references event hooks in curam.servicedelivery.impl.ServiceDeliveryEstimatedCost and curam.servicedelivery.impl.ServiceDelivery, including before/after events for determining service offering rates, determining rates per service occurrence date, and submitting a service delivery. Useful if you need extension points or event class names/methods for service delivery cost estimation or submission.

Lists Service Delivery customization points in Cúram 8.1.2, specifically event hooks for service delivery. It covers before/after events in curam.servicedelivery.impl.ServiceDeliveryEstimatedCost for determining service offering rates and rates per service occurrence date, plus curam.servicedelivery.impl.ServiceDelivery.submit for service delivery submission. Useful if you need extension points, event class names, or method references for customizing cost estimation or submission behavior.

Introduces CPM business events as before/after extension hooks for custom processing. It catalogs event classes and trigger methods across provider management, including deprecated Provider, ProviderApprovalCheck, ProviderEnquiry, License, HomeStudy, Compartment, Place, Request, MemberCertification, ProviderDeduction, ProviderOffering, and ProviderOfferingRate. It also covers provider group and associate hooks, plus nearby event sections for service delivery, financial, and referral customization. Open this node for a broad index of event hook names and lifecycle methods.

children.title: 1.3 CPM Workflow Process Definitions
Introduction
children.summary: Introduces Cúram Provider Management workflow process definitions and notes that agencies can copy and customize the included workflows, with custom versions taking precedence over installed ones. It begins the External Enquiry Workflow section, explaining that this workflow is enacted when an external party uses CPM to inquire about becoming a provider, and is triggered by `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalProviderEnquiry.createEnquiry`. It also gives the source XML location for the workflow.

children.title: External Enquiry Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: Describes the External Enquiry Workflow in Cúram Provider Management. It is enacted when an external party uses CPM to ask about becoming a provider, such as via an external-facing system. The workflow is triggered by `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalProviderEnquiry.createEnquiry`, and the section also gives the source XML location: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/EXTERNALENQUIRYWORKFLOW_v1.xml`.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section lists the source location for the External Enquiry workflow XML in CPM: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/EXTERNALENQUIRYWORKFLOW_v1.xml`. It sits within the Provider Management workflow documentation, which explains that included workflows can be copied and customized, and that custom versions take precedence.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: This section describes the default behavior of two Cúram CPM workflows. The provider inquiry workflow creates a manual activity, allocated by a function allocation strategy to the provider inquiry work queue, where a reviewer can transfer the inquiry to an enrolled provider or close it. The home study approval workflow creates a manual approval activity, by default routed to the submitting user’s supervisor, though agencies can change this. It also notes the related event details and event-notification links.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section gives event detail notation for the External Enquiry workflow. It lists the events raised for transferring or closing a provider enquiry, including the primary data field providerEnquiryID and the source methods in ProviderEnquiry. It also begins the Home Study Approval workflow section, describing its enactment on home study submission, source location, default manual approval activity, allocation to a supervisor, and reviewer actions to approve or reject.

Explains the CPM External Enquiry Workflow, which is triggered when an external party uses Cúram Provider Management to inquire about becoming a provider. It gives an example use case and identifies the enactment method, `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalProviderEnquiry.createEnquiry`. It also provides the workflow XML source location: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/EXTERNALENQUIRYWORKFLOW_v1.xml`.

children.title: Home Study Approval Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: Home Study Approval workflow: triggered when a user submits a home study for approval, via `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.homestudy.impl.HomeStudyImpl.submit`. It creates a manual approval activity allocated by function allocation strategy, defaulting to the submitter’s supervisor, though agencies can reroute it. The reviewer can approve or reject, after which the workflow completes. The section also points to the workflow XML source location (`EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/HOMESTUDYAPPROVAL_v1.xml`) and begins the event details table.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This page shows the source location for the Home Study Approval workflow XML file: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/HOMESTUDYAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. It also describes the workflow’s default behavior: when a home study is submitted for approval, a manual activity is created and allocated by a function allocation strategy, typically to the submitter’s supervisor. The reviewer can approve or reject the request, and the workflow completes afterward.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: This section describes the default behavior of the Home Study Approval workflow. It explains that when a home study is submitted for approval, the workflow creates a manual activity assigned via a function allocation strategy, by default routing it to the submitting user’s supervisor. The reviewer can approve or reject the request, after which the workflow completes. It also notes that agencies can customize the allocation target, such as routing to another user or group, and references event details for the workflow.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section lists event details for the External Enquiry workflow. It shows the raised events and primary data for transferring or closing a provider inquiry, including the providerEnquiryID and the related ProviderEnquiry methods. It also begins the Home Study Approval workflow description, noting that approval is triggered when a home study is submitted, the source location of the workflow XML, and that a manual activity is created for supervisor approval or rejection.

Home Study Approval Workflow explains that a home study submitted via `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.homestudy.impl.HomeStudyImpl.submit` triggers a workflow that creates a manual approval activity. By default, the function allocation strategy routes it to the submitter’s supervisor, though agencies can change the assignee to another user or group. The reviewer can approve or reject the request, after which the workflow completes. The section also gives the XML source location: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/HOMESTUDYAPPROVAL_v1.xml`, and starts the event details area.

children.title: New Invoice Created Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: This section describes the New Invoice Created workflow. It explains that the workflow is enacted whenever an external user submits an invoice for processing, and is triggered from curam.cpm.facade.impl.Request.createFinancialsTask. It links to the workflow XML location (EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/NEWINVOICECREATED_v1.xml), outlines the default behavior of creating a manual activity to route the invoice to a financial user, and includes event details for invoice submitted/cancelled events and their raised-from methods.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section covers the “Source Location” for the New Invoice Created workflow, including the XML file path: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/NEWINVOICECREATED_v1.xml`. It explains the workflow’s purpose and default behavior: when an external user submits an invoice, the system creates a manual activity to assign it for processing, typically to a financial user via a function allocation strategy. It also lists event details, including `NEWINVOICECREATED.INVOICECANCELLED` and `NEWINVOICECREATED.INVOICESUBMITTED`, with their primary data and originating methods.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: This section describes the default behavior of the New Invoice Created workflow. It explains that when an external user submits an invoice, the workflow creates a manual activity to assign the invoice to a financial user for processing, using a function allocation strategy. It notes that agencies may change this behavior to route invoices to a different user. The section also links to the workflow’s enactment source, XML location, and event details, including invoice submitted and cancelled events.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section lists event details for workflows. It includes Home Study Approval events and the New Invoice Created workflow, with raised events, primary event data, and source methods. For New Invoice Created, it explains when the workflow is enacted (on external invoice submission), where the XML definition is located, and its default behavior of creating a manual activity to assign the invoice to a financial user. It also introduces the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow.

Defines the New Invoice Created workflow and its event details. It is enacted when an external user submits an invoice for processing, triggered from `curam.cpm.facade.impl.Request.createFinancialsTask`. The workflow XML is located at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/NEWINVOICECREATED_v1.xml`. By default, it creates a manual activity to route the invoice to a financial user via a function allocation strategy, though agencies can change the assignee. Event details include `NEWINVOICECREATED.INVOICECANCELLED` and `NEWINVOICECREATED.INVOICESUBMITTED` with `serviceInvoiceID` as primary data.

children.title: Service Invoice Exception Processing Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: Defines the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow. It is enacted when a service invoice line item lacks sufficient correct data to match its service authorization, and is called from `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl.processInvoiceLineItem`. The default behavior creates a manual activity for a financial user to review mismatched invoice details, allocated via a function allocation strategy to the invoice exception processing work queue. It also lists related event details for processed/canceled/denied SILIs and begins the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow section.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: Covers the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow, triggered when a service invoice line item lacks enough correct data to match its service authorization and is called from `ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl.processInvoiceLineItem`. The section gives the XML location `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SERVICEINVOICEEXCEPTIONPROCESSING_v1.xml`, explains default behavior (creates a manual review activity routed to the invoice exception processing work queue), notes possible reallocation via function allocation strategy, and lists related event details and raised events. It also begins the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow section.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior of the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow: when a service invoice line item lacks sufficient correct data to match its service authorization, the workflow creates a manual review activity for a financial user, allocated to the invoice exception processing work queue by default. The reviewer can amend and resubmit the SILI or deny/cancel it, after which the workflow ends. Also notes the modeled allocation function, related event details, and introduces the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: Lists event details for the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow. It shows the raised events and payload for invoice line item review actions: PROVIDERMANAGEMENT.SILIPROCESSED, SILICANCELED, and SILIDENIED, each carrying serviceInvoiceLineItemID and raised from ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl. The section follows the workflow’s default behavior: a manual activity for resolving invoice line items that do not match the associated service authorization, typically allocated to the invoice exception processing work queue.

Defines the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow. It is triggered when a service invoice line item (SILI) lacks enough correct data to match its service authorization and is invoked from `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl.processInvoiceLineItem`. The default behavior creates a manual review activity for a financial user, usually allocated to the invoice exception processing work queue via a function allocation strategy. The section also lists related events for processed, canceled, and denied SILIs, with `serviceInvoiceLineItemID`, and begins the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow section.

children.title: Service Invoice Line Item Approval Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: Describes the Service Invoice Exception Processing workflow: it is enacted when insufficient correct data prevents matching a service invoice line item to its service authorization. Called from `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl.processInvoiceLineItem`, it creates a manual review activity for invoice exception processing, typically assigned to a financial user’s work queue via a function allocation strategy. The reviewer can amend, deny, or cancel the SILI, after which the workflow completes. Includes the workflow XML location and event details for SILI processed, canceled, and denied events.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section lists source file locations for Service Invoice Line Item approval workflows and describes their default behavior. It explains that the approval workflow creates a manual activity for a financial user to approve or deny a service invoice line item, normally routed to the invoice exception processing work queue via a function allocation strategy, with possible customization. It also documents the related event details for approved/denied outcomes and notes the correction approval workflow activation and XML file location.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior of the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow: it creates a manual approval/denial activity for a service invoice line item, assigned by default to the invoice exception processing work queue via the function allocation strategy `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.impl.WorkflowAllocationFunction.siliExceptionProcessing`. Agencies can redirect it to another queue or user. The section also lists event details for `PROVIDERMANAGEMENT.SILIAPPROVED` and `PROVIDERMANAGEMENT.SILIDENIED`, and introduces the Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval workflow and its source XML location.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: Defines the event details for the Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow. It lists two raised events—PROVIDERMANAGEMENT.SILIAPPROVED and PROVIDERMANAGEMENT.SILIDENIED—both carrying serviceInvoiceLineItemID and raised from curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl. The section also notes the workflow’s default behavior: a manual approval/denial activity assigned to the invoice exception processing work queue, with a configurable allocation strategy. It begins the related Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval workflow and points to its XML source location.

Introduces the Service Invoice Line Item Approval Workflow. It is enacted when a service invoice line item reaches “Pending Approval” after successful processing and is called from `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemImpl.enactSILIApprovalWorkflow`. The section points to the workflow XML source location and notes that this approval flow follows the same manual review pattern as invoice exception processing: a financial user is assigned an approval/denial activity via a function allocation strategy, with configurable routing and related event details for approval outcomes.

children.title: Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: This section says the Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval workflow is enacted when a service invoice line item correction is submitted for approval, and it is invoked by `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemCorrectionImpl.enactCorrectionApprovalWo...`. It also identifies the workflow XML location as `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SERVICEINVOICELINEITEMCORRECTIONAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. The surrounding text also describes the related Service Invoice Line Item Approval workflow’s default manual approval activity, allocation strategy, and event details.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section gives the source file locations for the Service Invoice Line Item Approval and Correction Approval workflows. It explains the default approval behavior: a manual activity is created, routed to the invoice exception processing work queue, and can be approved or denied by a financial user. It also notes the allocation strategy used and lists the main approval/denial event details and the method that enacts correction approval.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes default workflow behavior for service invoice line item correction approval: a manual activity is created and assigned via a function allocation strategy, by default routing to the submitting user’s supervisor, though agencies can change the assignee. The reviewer can approve or deny, and the workflow ends after completion. Also lists event details for approval/denial events and introduces the Supervisor Request Decision workflow, which creates a manual activity sending an external user account request to an administrator for setup as a provider member, participant, or group member.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section lists event details for the Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval workflow, including the raised events for approval/denial and the primary data passed. It also introduces the Supervisor Request Decision workflow, explaining when it is enacted, the calling method, source XML location, and default behavior for routing external user account requests to an administrator for setup.

This section covers the Service Invoice Line Item Correction Approval workflow. It states the workflow is enacted when a service invoice line item correction is submitted for approval, called by `curam.financial.impl.ServiceInvoiceLineItemCorrectionImpl.enactCorrectionApprovalWo...`, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SERVICEINVOICELINEITEMCORRECTIONAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. The excerpt also includes adjacent approval-workflow details: manual approval activity, allocation to invoice exception processing via a function allocation strategy, approval/denial outcomes, and event details for service invoice line item approval/denial.

children.title: Supervisor Request Decision Workflow
Enacted From
children.summary: Describes the Supervisor Request Decision workflow’s enactment and purpose: it runs when a user submits a request to create an external user account, via `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalRequests.submitRequest`. It creates a manual activity for an administrator to review the request for an external user to be added as a provider member, provider participant, or provider group member. Includes the source XML location: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SUPERVISORREQUESTDECISION_v1.xml`.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: Lists the source location for the Supervisor Request Decision workflow XML file: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SUPERVISORREQUESTDECISION_v1.xml`. Context indicates this workflow is triggered when a user submits a request to create an external user account, and it creates a manual activity for an administrator to approve setup as a provider member, provider participant, or provider group member.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes default workflow behavior for service invoice line item correction approval and supervisor request decision. The first workflow creates a manual approval activity, normally routed to the submitting user’s supervisor via `WorkflowAllocationFunction.siliExceptionProcessing`, with approval/denial ending the workflow and raising corresponding events. The second workflow is triggered by external user account setup requests (`ExternalRequests.submitRequest`) and creates a manual activity for an administrator to decide whether to add the external user as a provider member, provider participant, or provider group member.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section documents workflow event details for Supervisor Request Decision, listing two primary events: REQUESTDECISION.REQUESTACCEPTED and REQUESTDECISION.REQUESTREJECTED, both carrying requestID and raised by request accept/reject methods. It also introduces the Supervisor View New External User Task Notification workflow, describing its purpose, trigger when an admin creates an external user account, default notification behavior, source XML location, and noting that no events are raised for this workflow.

Describes the Supervisor Request Decision workflow, triggered by `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalRequests.submitRequest` when a user requests an external account. It creates a manual activity for an administrator to review whether the external user should be set up as a provider member, provider participant, or provider group member. The section also gives the workflow XML source location: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/SUPERVISORREQUESTDECISION_v1.xml`.

children.title: Supervisor View New External User Task Notification Workflow
Enacted from
children.summary: Describes the “Supervisor View New External User Task Notification” workflow. It is enacted when an administrator creates an external user account and is called from `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalUser.createExternalUser`. The workflow’s default behavior creates a route activity that notifies the owner/resource manager of the new external user, but agencies can redirect the notification to another user. The workflow ends after the notification activity completes. No events are raised.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section gives the source location and behavior of the Supervisor View New External User Task Notification workflow. It identifies the XML file path, explains that the workflow runs when an administrator creates an external user account, and says it routes a notification to the external user’s owner, defaulting to the resource manager who enrolled the user. It also notes agencies can change the default routing, and that no events are raised for this workflow.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior of the “Supervisor View New External User Task Notification” workflow. When an administrator creates a new external user account, the workflow creates a route activity that notifies the external user’s owner, by default the resource manager who enrolled the user, that the account was created successfully. Agencies can change the notification recipient. The section also notes that no events are raised and the workflow completes after the activity finishes.

children.title: Events Details
children.summary: This section lists event notation for the Supervisor Request Decision workflow, showing two raised events: REQUESTDECISION.REQUESTACCEPTED and REQUESTDECISION.REQUESTREJECTED, both carrying requestID and raised by request accept/reject methods. It also describes the Supervisor View New External User Task Notification workflow: triggered when an administrator creates an external user account, called from ExternalUser.createExternalUser, with default behavior sending a notification to the resource manager and no events raised.

Defines the “Supervisor View New External User Task Notification” workflow. It runs when an administrator creates a new external user account, via `curam.cpm.eua.facade.impl.ExternalUser.createExternalUser`. The workflow creates a route activity to notify the external user’s owner, defaulting to the resource manager who enrolled the user, though agencies can redirect the notification to another user. The workflow completes after the notification activity finishes, and no events are raised.

children.title: Roster Exception Processing Workflow
Enacted From
children.summary: Defines when the Roster Exception Processing workflow starts: when a provider roster line item lacks sufficient correct data to match its service authorization. Notes the calling method, source XML location, and default behavior: create a manual activity for review, typically routed to the roster exception processing work queue via a function allocation strategy. The reviewer may change and resubmit the line item or deny/cancel it, ending the workflow. Also lists related events raised: ROSTER.PRLI_PROCESSED, ROSTER.PRLI_CANCELED, and ROSTER.PRLI_DENIED.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section gives the Source Location for the Roster Exception Processing workflow XML and explains the workflow’s purpose, default behavior, and event details. It is triggered when a roster line item cannot be matched to its service authorization due to insufficient or incorrect data. The workflow creates a manual review activity, usually assigned to the roster exception processing work queue, where a user can modify, deny, or cancel the roster line item. It also lists related events such as PRLI_PROCESSED, PRLI_CANCELED, and PRLI_DENIED.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior of the Roster Exception Processing workflow: when roster line data cannot be matched to its service authorization, it creates a manual review activity for a user. The activity is normally assigned to the roster exception processing work queue via a function allocation strategy, but agencies can route it elsewhere. The reviewer can amend and resubmit the roster line item or deny/cancel it, after which the workflow ends. Also lists related event details and the workflow XML/source location.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: Lists the event details for the Roster Exception Processing workflow. It covers the events raised when a provider roster line item is processed, canceled, or denied: ROSTER.PRLI_PROCESSED, ROSTER.PRLI_CANCELED, and ROSTER.PRLI_DENIED. Each event carries providerRosterLineItemID as primary event data and is raised from the relevant roster/service invoice implementation classes. This section is useful if you need the workflow’s event notation and triggering points.

Defines the Roster Exception Processing workflow, triggered when a provider roster line item lacks sufficient correct data to match its service authorization. It notes the call from ProviderRosterLineItemImpl.processRosterLineItem, the workflow XML location, and default behavior: create a manual review activity, usually assigned to the roster exception processing work queue via a function allocation strategy. The reviewer can amend and resubmit or deny/cancel the line item, ending the workflow. Also lists events ROSTER.PRLI_PROCESSED, ROSTER.PRLI_CANCELED, and ROSTER.PRLI_DENIED with providerRosterLineItemID.

children.title: New Client Added to Roster Workflow
Enacted From
children.summary: This section explains when the “New Client Added to Roster” workflow and the “Roster Line Item Approval” workflow are triggered, and where their XML definitions are located. It notes that the first workflow runs when a provider roster line item is created during service authorization line item creation/modification, sends notifications to the roster owner, and removes them if the item is submitted, canceled, or denied. It also states the approval workflow starts when a roster line item needs manual approval and reaches Pending Approval.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section lists workflow source locations and behaviors for provider management. It covers the New Client Added to Roster workflow, triggered when a provider roster line item is created during service authorization creation/modification, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/NEWCLIENTADDEDTOROSTER_v1.xml`. It also describes event handling for roster processed/canceled/denied states. The section then introduces the Roster Line Item Approval workflow, enacted when a roster line item needs manual approval and is pending approval, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/ROSTERLINEITEMAPPROVAL_v1.xml`.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior of the New Client Added to Roster workflow: it creates a notification activity for the owner of a provider roster line item when a client is added during service authorization creation/modification that generates a roster line item. The notification is removed if the roster line item is submitted, canceled, or denied. Also lists related event details and points to the workflow XML location, with a transition into the Roster Line Item Approval workflow.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section documents event details for the “New Client Added to Roster” workflow. It explains when the workflow is enacted, where it is called from, its XML source location, and its default behavior for creating and removing notification activities tied to roster line items. It also lists the specific events raised or processed, their primary data field, and the originating classes. The section then begins the next topic, “Roster Line Item Approval Workflow,” including when it is enacted and its source XML location.

This section introduces the Roster Exception Processing workflow, which is triggered when a provider roster line item cannot be matched to its service authorization due to insufficient correct data. It identifies the calling method, XML source location, default behavior, and event details for processed/canceled/denied roster events. It explains that the workflow creates a manual review activity allocated to a roster exception processing work queue, allowing changes, denial, or cancellation. The section then begins the next topic: New Client Added to Roster Workflow.

children.title: Roster Line Item Approval Workflow
Enacted From
children.summary: This section explains when two provider management workflows are triggered and where they are defined. It covers the “New Client Added to Roster” workflow, enacted when a roster line item is created during service authorization line item creation/modification, including its notification behavior and related event details. It also introduces the “Roster Line Item Approval” workflow, enacted when a roster line item requires manual approval and reaches Pending Approval, with its call source and XML location.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section lists workflow source locations and behavior for provider roster line items. It covers the “New Client Added to Roster” workflow, triggered during service authorization line item creation/modification when a roster line item is created, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/NEWCLIENTADDEDTOROSTER_v1.xml`. It also covers the “Roster Line Item Approval” workflow, enacted when a roster line item needs manual approval and reaches Pending Approval, called from `ProviderRosterLineItemImpl.approve`, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/ROSTERLINEITEMAPPROVAL_v1.xml`.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior for the Roster Line Item Approval and Correction Approval workflows. Both create a manual review activity for approving or denying a provider roster line item or correction. By default, the activity is allocated to the roster exception processing work queue via the function allocation strategy `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.intf.WorkflowAllocationFunction.prliExceptionProcessing`, though agencies can change the queue. The workflow completes after the reviewer approves or denies the item. Includes event details for approval/denial events.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: This section documents event details for the Roster Line Item Approval workflow, including the raised events ROSTER.PRLI_APPROVED and ROSTER.PRLI_DENIED, both carrying providerRosterLineItemID and raised from curam.attendance.impl.ProviderRosterLineItemImp. It also introduces the Roster Line Item Correction Approval workflow, describing its enactment from PRLICorrectionImpl.approve, source XML location, and default behavior: a manual review activity routed via function allocation to the roster exception processing work queue for approve/deny completion.

This section documents the Roster Line Item Approval workflow: when it is triggered, its source XML, and its related behavior. It says the workflow starts when a provider roster line item requires manual approval and reaches Pending Approval, called from `curam.attendance.impl.ProviderRosterLineItemImpl.approve`, with XML at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/ROSTERLINEITEMAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. The page also includes the preceding New Client Added to Roster workflow details, including enactment, source location, default notification behavior, and event details.

children.title: Roster Line Item Correction Approval Workflow
Enacted From
children.summary: This section explains when the Roster Line Item Correction Approval workflow is started: whenever a user approves a provider roster line item correction, via `curam.attendance.impl.PRLICorrectionImpl.approve`. It also notes the workflow’s source XML location and summarizes its default behavior: it creates a manual review activity, typically routed to the roster exception processing work queue through `WorkflowAllocationFunction.prliExceptionProcessing`, where the reviewer can approve or deny the correction before the workflow completes.

children.title: Source Location
children.summary: This section gives the source location for the Roster Line Item Correction Approval workflow XML: `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/ROSTERLINEITEMCORRECTIONAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. It explains that the workflow is invoked when a user approves a provider roster line item correction, via `curam.attendance.impl.PRLICorrectionImpl.approve`. The default behavior creates a manual review activity routed to the roster exception processing work queue using `WorkflowAllocationFunction.prliExceptionProcessing`, where the reviewer approves or denies the correction and then the workflow completes.

children.title: Default Behavior
children.summary: Describes the default behavior for provider roster line item approval and correction approval workflows. Both create a manual review activity to approve or deny the item/correction, with the default assignment routed to the roster exception processing work queue via function allocation strategy `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.intf.WorkflowAllocationFunction.prliExceptionProcessing`. Notes that the workflow completes after the reviewer’s decision. Also includes event details for approval/denial events and their primary data, plus the correction workflow entry point and source XML location.

children.title: Event Details
children.summary: Defines the event-detail notation for the Roster Line Item Correction Approval workflow. It lists two events: ROSTER.PRLIC_APPROVED and ROSTER.PRLIC_DENIED, both carrying primary event data prliCorrectionID and raised by PRLICorrectionImpl.approv/deny. The page then begins CPM Products and Rule Sets, explaining that Provider Management uses four configurable products (Invoice, Placement, Contract, Attendance) to drive background financial processing for provider-related events, and notes related DMX files.

Describes the Roster Line Item Correction Approval workflow, triggered when a user approves a provider roster line item correction via `curam.attendance.impl.PRLICorrectionImpl.approve`. It points to the XML source at `EJBServer/components/CPM/workflow/ROSTERLINEITEMCORRECTIONAPPROVAL_v1.xml`. Default behavior creates a manual review activity routed to the roster exception processing work queue using `curam.cpm.workflowprocesses.intf.WorkflowAllocationFunction.prliExceptionProcessing`; the reviewer approves or denies the correction, then the workflow completes.

Defines CPM workflow process definitions and notes agencies can copy/customize them, with custom versions overriding installed ones. Covers multiple built-in workflows: External Enquiry, Home Study Approval, New Invoice Created, Service Invoice Exception Processing, Service Invoice Line Item Approval/Correction Approval, Supervisor Request Decision, Supervisor View New External User Task Notification, Roster Exception Processing, New Client Added to Roster, Roster Line Item Approval, and Roster Line Item Correction Approval. For each, it gives the trigger method, default manual/route activity behavior, assignee/allocation strategy, XML source location, and event details where applicable.

children.title: 1.4 CPM Products and Rule Sets
Products
children.summary: Explains Cúram Provider Management products and notification customization. The four products—Provider Invoice, Placement, Contract, and Attendance—generate financials in the background and are excluded from standard notifications. Describes the immutable DMX files involved, how to extend NotificationExcludedProviderCaseTypesImpl to add or change excluded product delivery/case types, and how to bind the custom implementation. Also covers how workflows CASEREASSESSMENTNOTIFICATION and DEFAULTCASENOTIFICATION use the exclusion interface via allocation strategies, with sample code.

children.title: Rule Sets
children.summary: Lists CPM rule sets and their XML source locations for four payment types: Provider Invoice, Provider Placement, Flat-Rate Contract Payments, and Provider Attendance Payments. Notes that rule sets may be customized only if they do not depend on new evidence types. Also introduces CPM financials, which use the Classic Assessment/reassessment framework, evidence functionality, and Classic Rules Engine to support maintenance, evidence management, rule execution, assessments, payment generation, and platform financial processing.

Explains CPM financial products and notification customization. It defines four background-only products—Provider Invoice, Placement, Contract, and Attendance—used to generate payments, notes their immutable DMX files, and says customization is done via extension points rather than DMX edits. It also shows how to extend NotificationExcludedProviderCaseTypesImpl and bind it to exclude additional case/product delivery types from notifications. Finally, it describes how CASEREASSESSMENTNOTIFICATION and DEFAULTCASENOTIFICATION use this exclusion logic through allocation strategies, with sample code.

children.title: 1.5 CPM Financials
Payment Types
Service Invoice
children.summary: Explains service invoice processing in Provider Management: service authorizations support invoicing by multiple providers, invoices are paid after approval, and line-item payments become evidence. Describes related product delivery cases for Provider Invoice, Placement, and Flat Rate Contract, including when each case is created, how evidence and payment schedules are set, and how placement cases handle transfers. Also introduces attendance rosters for Attendance Tracking, where client attendance is recorded for delivered services.

children.title: Placement
children.summary: This section explains placement-related provider management in Cúram/SEM. It describes how a placement service authorizes a client’s physical placement with a provider, with financials starting from day one and days as the unit of measure. It explains that placement details become evidence, and a Provider Placement product delivery case is created for each placement and when a client transfers within a facility. It also notes the delivery pattern comes from CPM administration and gives an example of case creation and payment timing.

children.title: Flat rate contract
children.summary: A Flat Rate Contract is a formal agreement between a Provider and the SEM agency for one or more services. Contract details are treated as evidence for financial processing. When a flat rate contract is first activated, a Provider Contract product delivery case is created; one PD case is created per contract per provider, and a new one is created on renewal. The contract information is used to set the provider’s payment schedule.

children.title: Attendance
children.summary: This page explains provider payment processing for service invoices, placements, and flat rate contracts, then starts the Attendance topic. Attendance rosters are used when delivered services require client attendance to be tracked and reported through Attendance Tracking. The excerpt is introductory and does not include the full attendance details.

Introduces CPM payment types and related rule sets, then explains four provider payment models: Service Invoice, Placement, Flat Rate Contract, and Attendance. Service invoices are paid after approval, with line-item evidence and one Provider Invoice PD case per provider/payee. Placement starts financials from day one, uses days as the unit, and creates Provider Placement PD cases per placement or transfer. Flat rate contracts create Provider Contract PD cases on activation/renewal and define payment schedules. Attendance begins the topic of rosters for tracking client attendance.

CPM Financials describes how provider payments are built on the Classic Assessment/reassessment framework, evidence, and rules engine. It outlines four payment types: Service Invoice, Placement, Flat Rate Contract, and Attendance. Service invoices pay after approval via Provider Invoice PD cases and rolled-up payments. Placement starts financials from day one using days as the unit and creates Provider Placement PD cases per placement/transfer. Flat rate contracts create Provider Contract PD cases on activation/renewal and set payment schedules. Attendance begins roster-based attendance tracking.

children.title: 1.6 Service Deliveries
Product Design and Configuration
children.summary: Explains how service deliveries can be configured to use product delivery processing, CPM processing, or both within an integrated case or outcome plan. Covers provider attendance roster payments: rosters may be submitted internally or externally, generated automatically by frequency, approved to create a Provider Attendance product delivery case, and paid as rolled-up period payments based on configured payment frequency. Notes that if product delivery is used for eligibility or payment, a corresponding product must be configured with appropriate CER rules and rate tables.

children.title: Rule Set Creation
children.summary: This section explains how to configure rule sets for eligibility and payment calculations in provider management. It recommends using separate objectives for eligibility (non-financial, e.g. Double) and payment (financial, Money), with the payment objective depending on eligibility and evidence such as invoices or attendance. It also covers evidence configuration and propagation types, shared evidence usage, recalculation on evidence changes, and how custom rates require a rate table linked to the rule set, including populating decision values and estimated cost.

children.title: Evidence and Evidence Maintenance
children.summary: This section explains how to configure evidence for rule-set calculations in provider management. It covers using client, case, service, invoice, and attendance evidence for eligibility and payment objectives, including proper Objective Tag Type value types (non-money for eligibility, Money for payment).
