It also describes required propagator types for evidence entities, the role of shared evidence at the integrated case level, how evidence changes trigger reassessment, and how custom rates/rate tables can be used to populate decision values and estimated cost.

children.title: Custom Rates
children.summary: Explains how custom rates are used in CER rule sets to calculate service payment amounts. A rate table must be created and associated with the rule set, and the Case Decision Objective’s value should be populated from those rates. Notes that CPM values such as invoice or roster amounts can also be used, and an Estimated Cost attribute may be included when custom rates replace the default CPM calculation.

children.title: Service Delivery Creation
children.summary: Creating service deliveries can automatically generate an underlying, hidden product delivery case for types like Product Delivery, Product Delivery with Invoicing, and Service Delivery with Eligibility. Its case ID is stored on the service delivery and linked to related evidence records. For eligibility-based product deliveries, Assessment Engine events can auto-create service authorizations when eligible. A public API exists to create a service delivery. The section also notes that product delivery information can be displayed at service-delivery level via client navigation configuration.

children.title: Display of Product Delivery Information
children.summary: Product delivery-related information (eligibility, financial transactions, determinations, evidence) is automatically shown at the service delivery level and can be viewed by case workers there. Other product delivery details, such as certification and appeal information, can also be displayed, but require additional development and configuration via client navigation files.

This section explains service deliveries in integrated cases or outcome plans, and how they can use product delivery, CPM processing, or both. It covers product/rule-set setup for eligibility and payment, including separate CER objectives, evidence types and propagators, shared evidence, reassessment on evidence changes, and custom rate tables. It also notes provider attendance rosters: approvals create a Provider Attendance product delivery case, evidence drives CPM payment calculation, and payments are rolled up by frequency.

children.title: 1.7 Compliancy for Provider Manager
Miscellaneous Entities
children.summary: This section lists miscellaneous CPM entities introduced to support multiple clients for provider roster line items: PRLIClient, PRLIClientHistory, and PRLICorrectionClient. It warns these entities are not currently supported by the application, may change in future, and are highly recommended against for use. The page then begins Appendix A with a Castor v0.9.5.4 XML mapping example.

This section says the CPM Sample component is only for testing CPM APIs and extension points and is not supported for use or customization. It identifies three prohibited packages, including curam.cpmsample.changecases.impl and curam.cpmsample.facade.impl, and notes the associated client directory is components/CPMSample. It is mainly a warning/constraint section rather than functional guidance.

children.title: 1.8 Appendix A
children.summary: Appendix A gives an XML mapping example based on the Castor v0.9.5.4 Mapping schema. It shows how taxonomy-related classes are bound to XML, including `Taxonomy`, `TaxonomyTerm`, `RelatedConcept`, and `ExternalTerm`, with fields mapped to elements, attributes, and collections such as terms, external terms, related concepts, use references, related terms, and old codes. Useful if you need the specific XML structure for CPM taxonomy serialization.

children.title: 1.9 Appendix B: Schema definitions of the XML fragments created by Import process.
The UseReference.xsd file
children.summary: Defines the UseReference.xsd schema for XML fragments created by the Import process. It shows a `useReferences` root containing one `useReference`, which in turn contains one `text` element. The `text` element extends `xs:string` and requires a `locale` attribute. Useful for understanding the structure and validation rules of use-reference XML output.

children.title: The RelatedConcept.xsd file
children.summary: This section begins Appendix B for XML schema definitions used by the Import process and introduces the RelatedConcept.xsd file. The visible content is mostly the start of the schema file, following the preceding UseReference.xsd example. It indicates that the document is providing XSD definitions for generated XML fragments, likely including the structure and required elements/attributes for related concept data.

children.title: The ExternalTerm.XSD file
children.summary: Defines the ExternalTerm.XSD schema. It shows an `externalTerm` element containing required `name` and `system` child elements and a required `code` attribute of type `xs:string`. The `name` element is mixed content with a required `locale` string attribute. The section also includes the document’s legal notices, copyright, privacy policy, and trademark information.

Appendix B defines XML schema fragments generated by the Import process. It includes XSDs for UseReference, RelatedConcept, and ExternalTerm. UseReference defines a useReferences root with one useReference containing text with required locale. RelatedConcept defines relatedConcept with required code and a name element with required locale. ExternalTerm defines externalTerm with required code plus name and system elements; name also requires locale. The section then transitions into legal notices, copyright, privacy, and trademark information.

This section introduces CPM customization vs configuration and explains the main extension points for provider management. It covers strategy-pattern interfaces and Guice replacement modules for provider enrollment, reference numbers, approvals, validation, placement/contract payment logic, training, and service invoice handling. It also references workflows, products/rule sets, and related customization areas. The section is useful as a high-level index for developers who need to locate specific CPM customization interfaces, default implementations, and extension points.
